title: Cumulative AI-related bills passed into law since 2016, as of 2024
subtitle: Bills passed into law by national legislative bodies (e.g., congress, parliament) with the keyword “artificial intelligence” (translated to the respective languages) in the title or body of the bill.
note: For example, the [Identifying Outputs of Generative Adversarial Networks Act](https://www.govinfo.gov/content/pkg/PLAW-116publ258/pdf/PLAW-116publ258.pdf) passed into law by the US Congress.
caption:
Cumulative AI-related bills passed into law since 2016, as of 2024

Bills passed into law by national legislative bodies (e.g., congress, parliament) with the keyword “artificial intelligence” (translated to the respective languages) in the title or body of the bill.

This choropleth world map displays the cumulative count of AI-related bills passed into law by national legislative bodies from 2016 through 2024. The legend categorizes countries into six groups: No data (hatched), 0, 1, 2, 5-10, and 10+. The United States and Russia are shaded in the darkest blue, indicating they fall into the highest category of 10 or more bills; for instance, the Identifying Outputs of Generative Adversarial Networks Act passed into law by the US Congress is one such example. Spain also appears in this darkest band. A mid-range blue shade, representing 5 to 10 bills, covers China, Argentina, the United Kingdom, France, and South Korea. Lighter blue shades indicating 1 or 2 bills are visible in countries such as Canada, Brazil, India, Japan, and Australia. Large portions of the map, including most of Africa, the Middle East, and parts of South America and Asia, are marked with diagonal hatching to signify no data. Several other nations, including Mexico and Indonesia, appear in the lightest solid shade, corresponding to zero bills. The visual distribution shows a concentration of legislative activity in North America, Europe, and East Asia, while vast regions remain without recorded data in this specific dataset.
